page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for vector and matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7i-flex.4xlarge demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and compression tasks. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 33.24 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieved 13.55 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Geekbench single-core performance scored 2045.0, also representing a strong tier result. Multi-core performance, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 12124.0 and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 16105.87, aligns with the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show a cached read-only speed of 1332.05 GB/sec and uncached write speeds of 74.81 GB/sec.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the flex designation indicates a balance of dedicated compute power with a lower entry cost compared to standard compute-optimized instances. However, tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage and a baseline network bandwidth limited to 3 Gbps. There are also no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

This instance is designed for workloads requiring moderate memory-to-CPU ratios and strong single-threaded execution. Suitable applications include web serving, software compilation, lightweight database operations, and small-scale language model inference, where the hardware's AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets can be leveraged.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. In benchmarks, it exhibits strong single-core performance, notably in text processing, gzip compression, and small-model LLM text generation, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This hardware profile is suited for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and compilation.
